hero.title: Radiant Waves Media Morning Global News
hero.description: Thursday, July 2, 2026. Good morning. Here are today's top verified headlines, FIFA World Cup updates and major developments from around the world.

breaking: 🔴 England defeat DR Congo 2-1 to qualify for the FIFA World Cup Round of 16
🔴 Belgium complete dramatic 3-2 comeback against Senegal after extra time
🔴 Portugal face Croatia, Spain meet Austria and Switzerland play Algeria today
🔴 France prepare for Round of 16 clash against Paraguay
🔴 Europe continues battling extreme heat across several countries
🔴 Nigeria sustains nationwide military operations against terrorists and kidnappers
🔴 Global markets remain focused on inflation, oil prices and interest rates
🔴 Artificial intelligence investment continues accelerating worldwide

featured.title: Belgium And England Progress As Knockout Drama Continues
featured.content: The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 delivered more drama as England came from behind to defeat DR Congo 2-1, while Belgium produced an extraordinary comeback from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time. Attention now shifts to another exciting day of knockout football.

articles.title: England Reach Round Of 16
articles.description: Harry Kane scored twice as England recovered from an early setback to defeat DR Congo 2-1 and secure a place in the Round of 16, where they will face Mexico.

articles.title: Belgium Complete Incredible Comeback
articles.description: Belgium overturned a two-goal deficit late in the match before defeating Senegal 3-2 after extra time in one of the most dramatic matches of the tournament.

articles.title: Senegal Exit After Heartbreaking Defeat
articles.description: Senegal were minutes away from victory before Belgium staged a remarkable comeback. The African side leave the tournament after a courageous performance.

articles.title: France Ready For Paraguay Challenge
articles.description: France continue preparations for their Round of 16 encounter against Paraguay after defeating Sweden 3-0. Ousmane Dembele and Kylian Mbappe remain among the tournament's standout performers.

articles.title: Today's FIFA World Cup Fixtures
articles.description: Portugal face Croatia, Spain meet Austria and Switzerland play Algeria as the Round of 32 continues across North America.

articles.title: Middle East Diplomacy Continues
articles.description: International diplomatic efforts continue as regional leaders engage in discussions aimed at improving security, humanitarian access and long-term stability.

articles.title: Europe Faces Persistent Heatwave
articles.description: Authorities across several European countries continue issuing heat alerts as exceptionally high temperatures increase health risks and wildfire concerns.

articles.title: Host Nations Welcome Knockout Stage
articles.description: The United States, Canada and Mexico continue hosting World Cup knockout matches as fan attendance and tourism remain exceptionally strong.

articles.title: China Expands AI And Manufacturing
articles.description: China continues investing in artificial intelligence, semiconductor production and advanced manufacturing to strengthen long-term economic competitiveness.

articles.title: Nigeria Sustains Security Operations
articles.description: Security agencies continue coordinated operations against insurgents, bandits and kidnapping gangs while authorities reaffirm commitments to improving national security.

articles.title: Economic Reforms Remain In Focus
articles.description: Inflation, food prices, electricity supply, tax reforms and infrastructure development remain key priorities in Nigeria's economic agenda.

articles.title: African Trade Cooperation Advances
articles.description: African countries continue strengthening regional trade, transportation links and investment partnerships to boost economic growth.

articles.title: Asia Continues Innovation Drive
articles.description: Governments across Asia continue expanding investment in artificial intelligence, digital infrastructure and advanced technology.

articles.title: Markets Watch Inflation And Energy Prices
articles.description: Global investors continue monitoring inflation, central bank policy decisions and crude oil prices as financial markets remain cautious.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence Race Accelerates
articles.description: Governments and technology companies continue increasing investment in AI, robotics, cloud computing and cybersecurity.

breakingHistory: England defeated DR Congo 2-1 to qualify for the Round of 16.
Belgium came from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time.
Portugal face Croatia in today's Round of 32 fixture.
Spain meet Austria while Switzerland take on Algeria.
France prepare to face Paraguay in the Round of 16.
Europe remains under severe heatwave warnings.
Global markets continue monitoring inflation and oil prices.
Artificial intelligence investment continues expanding worldwide.
